channel.title: Digital Wellness on SafeScreenFamily - Protect Your Kids Online
channel.description: Recent content in Digital Wellness on SafeScreenFamily - Protect Your Kids Online

item.title: Building a Digitally Healthy Family: Beyond Just Screen Time Limits
item.description:
If you’re reading this, you’ve probably already set up parental controls, configured screen time limits, and installed a content filter or two. And things are probably… still not great. Your kid is still glued to their devices. Dinner conversations still compete with notifications. Bedtime is still a nightly battle over “just five more minutes.”

Here’s the uncomfortable truth: screen time limits are necessary, but they’re not sufficient. They’re the equivalent of locking the cookie jar – it prevents the worst outcomes, but it doesn’t teach your kid how to make good food choices when the jar is open.

item.title: Screen Time Rules That Actually Work — A Parent's Guide by Age Group
item.description:
Screen Time Rules That Actually Work — A Parent’s Guide by Age Group

I used to think screen time management meant picking a number of minutes and yelling when the timer went off. That approach lasted about three days before everyone in the house was miserable, including me.

After years of trial and error, reading the research, and talking to other parents and pediatricians, I’ve landed on an approach that actually works for our family. The secret isn’t finding the perfect number of minutes — it’s building a structure that makes good habits automatic and bad habits harder.
